# Satya Nadella’s Post

[!\[\]()](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Satya Nadella](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

Satya Nadella is an Influencer

1h

- [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsatyanadella_excited-to-seeopen-to-workfrom-my-colleagues-activity-7444806163835236352-u1S0&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Excited to see Open to Work from my colleagues [Ryan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryanroslansky?trk=public_post-text) and [Aneesh](https://www.linkedin.com/in/aneeshraman?trk=public_post-text) out in the world. Ryan told me months ago he wanted to write a book to help people navigate this moment of profound change and thrive as AI reshapes how work gets done. I can’t think of two people better suited to write it. It’s a great guide for anyone trying to make sense of work right now.

[!\[\]()](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryanroslansky?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[Ryan Roslansky](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryanroslansky?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

Ryan Roslansky is an Influencer

21h

Today is the day - Open to Work is officially available! [https://lnkd.in/gDqY5Bna](https://lnkd.in/gDqY5Bna?trk=public_post_reshare-text)

[Aneesh](https://www.linkedin.com/in/aneeshraman?trk=public_post_reshare-text) and I are excited to share it with the world. But more than anything, today is about gratitude.

Gratitude for every past, present and future employee at LinkedIn and Microsoft who show up every day to create economic opportunity for the global workforce and to empower every person and organization in the world to achieve more. While that’s always true, it is especially true in moments of big change, when we all feel an extra responsibility to help professionals connect, learn, work, and grow in new ways, together.

Gratitude for the members and experts who helped us tell this story. Who helped us show that no matter where you live or what you do, no matter what kind of job title you hold or what kind of career trajectory you’re on, this story belongs to you.

Gratitude for the people - and the purpose - that made this book possible. Thank you.

[Ume Habiba](https://www.linkedin.com/in/umehabiba?trk=public_post_reshare-text), [JONETTA GRESHAM](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jonetta-gresham?trk=public_post_reshare-text), [Neil Open to Work is a timely guide for navigating the profound changes AI is bringing to the world of work.

From an ITSM and NOC consultancy perspective, the same principle applies in enterprise operations: success depends on frameworks that adapt to change, empower people, and ensure resilience. Just as AI reshapes how individuals thrive in their careers, AI-driven ITSM and NOC strategies reshape how enterprises thrive — enabling proactive monitoring, incident management, and measurable impact.

At D&A Technologies, we help organizations integrate AI into ITSM and NOC models, moving beyond reactive firefighting into proactive, trust-driven resilience. Whether in careers or IT ecosystems, the foundation of progress lies in adaptability, innovation, and systems that empower people.

#DandATechnologies #ITSM #NOC #DigitalTransformation #AI #Resilience #FutureOfWork

Big shift. New signals. Platform changes reshape incentives and redistribute access to opportunity at scale. I see this in job search too. Candidates who adapt early gain visibility while others stay invisible. For anyone navigating this, my friends at [Offered.ai](http://Offered.ai?trk=public_post_comment-text) connect candidates directly with recruiters and only get paid when you land a job, aligning incentives and reducing downside in the process. Lean into change and move before the crowd.

AI isn’t just reshaping how work gets done - it’s redefining the infrastructure required to support it.

The real bottleneck isn’t adoption. It’s the ability to deliver stable, high-quality power at scale - with the reliability, resilience, and safety these systems require.

The next phase of this transformation won’t be limited by software - it will be defined by whether the physical layer can keep up.

This couldn’t be more timely.

As Artificial Intelligence reshapes how work gets done, guidance like Open to Work becomes essential—not optional.

What makes this powerful is the focus on navigating change, not just understanding it:

→ From static careers → dynamic skill evolution → From job titles → capability-driven value → From linear growth → continuous reinvention

Huge credit to Ryan Roslansky and Aneesh Raman for addressing what so many professionals are feeling but struggling to articulate.

Because the real challenge today isn’t access to opportunity— it’s knowing how to adapt fast enough to capture it.

The future of work won’t reward what you know— it will reward how quickly you can evolve.

Title: Excited to see Open to Work from my colleagues Ryan and Aneesh out in the world. Ryan told me months ago he wanted to write a book to help people navigate this moment of profound change and thrive as AI reshapes how work gets done. I can’t think of two people better suited to write it. It’s a great guide for anyone trying to make sense of work right now.
